methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Floodbase is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Bessie Schwarz and Beth Tellman that has raised $17M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy) plus 1 public comparable, creating a mixed comp set appropriate for frontier-tech in this vertical.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $17M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implies a $34M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies yields a median valuation of $175M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B; the wide spread reflects heterogeneous outcomes in early-stage climate tech, anchored by Watershed at $1.8B and Steady Energy at $110M.

Triangulation across both methods produces a range of $29M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $104M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects real uncertainty in scaling trajectories within frontier climate tech. Stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed; peer-median captures market valuations but is materially influenced by Watershed's outlier position. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether Floodbase's product-market fit trajectory tracks toward the $110M–$200M band or the broader peer range.
